system_prompt: You are a master world-builder for a dark RPG. Your task is to generate a coherent, interconnected game world based on a provided Story Idea. The world must consist of unique scenes, connections (exits), NPCs, and interactable objects. Ensure the logic of the world is consistent: if a door is locked, mention why. Provide rich, atmospheric descriptions.
user_prompt:
Adventure Title: Hounted House II
Story Idea: In the shifting, non-Euclidean halls of Manor Rabenfels, a team of specialized "Extractors" is hired to recover a forbidden relic. This is not a standard ghost hunt: it is a psychological heist. The manor is a sentient predator that feeds on human memories, physically restructuring itself based on the occupants' pasts. To survive, the players must navigate distorted visions and trade their own identities for progress, eventually confronting their own mirror images. It is a race to secure the prize before the house consumes their reason for being there in the first place.

Generate at least 5 scenes with a complex network of exits and interesting entities.
response:
{"scenes":[
  {"id":"RABENFELS_GATE","name":"Iron Gate of Manor Rabenfels","description":"A black wrought-iron gate bows inward under the weight of age, its spear-point finials bent like broken teeth. Beyond it, Manor Rabenfels rises from a hill of wet stone and dead yew, its windows glowing with a dim, internal dusk. The air tastes of pennies and candle smoke. Every few seconds the manor shifts—an upstairs window appears where a chimney should be, then blinks away like a remembered lie."},
  {"id":"ENTRY_HALL","name":"The Entry Hall of Borrowed Names","description":"A grand foyer with a marble floor veined like a cracked skull and walls hung with portraits whose faces are scraped clean. The chandelier above is lit by pale blue flame and drips wax onto the floor in slow, deliberate pulses. Footsteps echo a half-second late, as if the house is deciding whether you deserve to have made them. Whispering seems to come from the walls, repeating names the listeners have not spoken in years."},
  {"id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","name":"Gallery of Unlived Lives","description":"A long corridor lined with frames, each holding a moving memory instead of a painting: a childhood winter that never happened, a wedding in an abandoned church, a battlefield seen through another person's eyes. The corridor subtly bends in impossible directions, and the farther one walks, the more the memories begin to overlap and rewrite one another. A faint scent of lavender and rot hangs in the air."},
  {"id":"SUNKEN_Library","name":"The Sunken Library","description":"A library that seems partially submerged in black water, though no ceiling leak can be found. Towers of books rise from the slick floor like gravestones, and the water reflects scenes that are not present in the room. Pages occasionally turn themselves, whispering in dry, papery breaths. At the center stands a reading desk nailed together from coffin planks."},
  {"id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","name":"The Mirror Chamber","description":"A circular room paneled in cracked mirror glass, each shard showing a slightly different version of the observer: older, younger, crueler, dead. The mirrors are not passive; they breathe with frost and occasionally smile before the viewer does. The room's geometry is wrong—one can cross the chamber in three steps or thirty, depending on what memory the house is tasting."},
  {"id":"HEART_VAULT","name":"The Heart Vault","description":"A vaulted chamber of wet stone and iron ribs, beating with a low, subterranean thrum. In its center rests the forbidden relic upon a pedestal grown from fused bone and lacquered wood. The walls are engraved with names, dates, and the beginnings of confessions that were never completed. The air is thick with the metallic odor of recent thoughts."},
  {"id":"CRAWLSPACE","name":"The Impossible Crawlspace","description":"A narrow void behind the walls where joists intersect at impossible angles and the darkness folds like cloth. Scratched messages run along the beams in many hands: warnings, apologies, directions that loop back on themselves. It is cold enough to numb memories of warmth, and every breath feels borrowed from someone else."}
],"exits":[
  {"from_scene_id":"RABENFELS_GATE","to_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","label":"through the creaking front doors and into the manor's breath","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","to_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","label":"up a staircase that appears where the west wall should be","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","to_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library","label":"through a velvet-draped archway that descends like a well","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The archway only opens to those carrying a remembered secret; the house seals it with a whispering lock of false voices."},
  {"from_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","to_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","label":"via a corridor reflected inside one of the memory frames","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","to_scene_id":"CRAWLSPACE","label":"through a section of wall that softens into upholstery and yields","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library","to_scene_id":"HEART_VAULT","label":"down a spiral stair hidden beneath the reading desk","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The stair is chained with iron script that can only be unbound by surrendering a cherished recollection to the library's waters."},
  {"from_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library","to_scene_id":"CRAWLSPACE","label":"through a flooded maintenance hatch behind the coffin-plank shelves","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","to_scene_id":"HEART_VAULT","label":"through the largest mirror, if your reflection permits passage","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The mirror admits only those whose reflected double agrees to exchange a piece of identity for entry."},
  {"from_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","to_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","label":"back down a hallway that appears only in glass","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"CRAWLSPACE","to_scene_id":"HEART_VAULT","label":"through a broken lath opening that leads beneath the manor's pulse","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},
  {"from_scene_id":"CRAWLSPACE","to_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","label":"up through a pantry chute that emerges behind a portrait wall","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null}
],"npcs":[
  {"id":"MRS_VON_RABENFELS","type":"NPC","name":"Widow Elsbeth von Rabenfels","description":"The manor's last living heir, a gaunt woman in mourning black with ink-stained fingers and a voice like paper tearing. She appears to know the house intimately, but speaks of it with the exhausted caution of someone describing a predator that has learned her name. She hires the Extractors yet seems unsure whether she wants the relic returned or the house put down.","start_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL"},
  {"id":"HOUSE_LECTOR","type":"NPC","name":"The House Lector","description":"An eyeless butler stitched into a formal coat, its throat lined with brass keys that click when it speaks. It recites inventories of lost items and forgotten visitors in a ceremonial monotone. It cannot lie, but it can omit entire rooms from conversation.","start_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL"},
  {"id":"YOUNG_EXTRACTOR_DOUBLE","type":"NPC","name":"The Younger Self","description":"A perfect mimic of one of the Extractors at an age ten years earlier, dressed in an old version of their own habits. It is charming, hopeful, and viciously honest in ways the living rarely survive. It knows intimate details no stranger should know because the house has fed it from the player's discarded memories.","start_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY"},
  {"id":"MIRROR_TWIN","type":"NPC","name":"The Mirror Twin","description":"A polished, patient doppelgänger formed from reflected intent rather than flesh. It moves a fraction of a second after its original and speaks only when it can improve upon a sentence. It seeks to complete the party's mission on its own terms and will gladly trade identities to do so.","start_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER"},
  {"id":"ARCHIVIST_DEAD","type":"NPC","name":"Archivist of Drowned Paper","description":"A waterlogged scholar in a collar of warped index cards, forever smelling of mildew and lamp oil. It rises from the flooded stacks to answer questions about the relic, but each answer costs a memory to the library's depths. Its hands are tattooed with shelf numbers instead of names.","start_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library"},
  {"id":"THE_RELIC_KEEPER","type":"NPC","name":"The Relic Keeper in the Walls","description":"A voice that speaks from behind plaster, sometimes a child, sometimes an elder, never the same twice. It claims to guard the forbidden relic and to be the oldest surviving part of the manor. It offers guidance, but every direction it gives makes the house grow more confident and hungry.","start_scene_id":"CRAWLSPACE"}
],"objects":[
  {"id":"FORBIDDEN_RELIC","type":"OBJECT","name":"The Forbidden Relic: Heart of Rabenfels","description":"A dark, fist-sized artifact of obsidian glass wrapped in a lattice of tarnished silver filigree. It seems to contain a moving interior scene: a tiny, endless hall where someone is always just out of sight. Proximity to it triggers recollections that do not belong to the holder, and prolonged exposure risks replacing one's own past with the relic's dead memories.","start_scene_id":"HEART_VAULT"},
  {"id":"MOURNING_KEY","type":"OBJECT","name":"Mourning Key","description":"A heavy iron key tied to a frayed black ribbon. It is warm to the touch, as if recently held. The key opens the false locks in the manor, but each use blurs one personal memory and leaves behind a faint smell of funeral lilies.","start_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL"},
  {"id":"HOUSE_LEDGER","type":"OBJECT","name":"Ledger of Borrowed Lives","description":"A leather-bound registry whose pages are filled with names written over and over in different handwriting styles, as though the manor keeps rehearsing ownership. Margins contain comments about appetites, fears, and preferred forms of despair. Reading it too long makes the reader suspect they are already listed somewhere inside.","start_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library"},
  {"id":"SPLINTERED_HAND_MIRROR","type":"OBJECT","name":"Splintered Hand Mirror","description":"A small silver hand mirror cracked into a spiderweb of shards. When angled correctly, it reveals not just reflections but the moment a memory was first lost. Some shards show the holder's reflection performing actions they have not yet taken.","start_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY"},
  {"id":"BONE_LATCH","type":"OBJECT","name":"Bone Latch Mechanism","description":"A skeletal locking apparatus fused into the base of the stair to the Heart Vault. Its vertebrae click in answer to spoken names, and it tightens when someone lies about what they want from the relic. It can be opened, but only by naming a thing one is prepared to sacrifice.","start_scene_id":"SUNKEN_Library"},
  {"id":"IDENTITY_TOKEN","type":"OBJECT","name":"Identity Token","description":"A thin brass disc stamped with a blank profile and a slot for a carved initial. The manor's older mechanisms accept it as payment for passage, but once used, the disc cools and the corresponding part of the user's self grows hazy and difficult to recall. The token must be inscribed with a piece of identity willingly abandoned.","start_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER"}
]}

Each shard shows a different emotional truth, none of them flattering. Looking into it too long can cause a person to remember events that happened to someone who looks exactly like them.","start_scene_id":"MIRROR_GALLERY","spatial_position":"resting on a velvet ottoman near the center of the gallery"},{"id":"ASH_KEY","type":"OBJECT","name":"Ash Key","description":"A key forged from compressed ash and iron filings, warm to the touch despite the cold air. It leaves a faint soot mark on the palm and smells like extinguished candles. The key fits doors that do not yet exist until certain vows are broken.","start_scene_id":"HEARTH_CHAMBER","spatial_position":"embedded in the cracked mantel above the dead firebox"},{"id":"MEMORY_VIALS","type":"OBJECT","name":"Memory Vials","description":"A set of narrow glass vials containing pearly light, each one tagged with a handwritten label: 'first fear,' 'mother's face,' 'the way home,' and others scratched out in haste. When uncorked, they release scenes, sounds, and emotions like trapped insects. The labels are not always truthful.","start_scene_id":"LIBRARY_OF_RECOLLECTION","spatial_position":"embedded in alcoves along the north wall"},{"id":"HOUSEHEART_BELL","type":"OBJECT","name":"Househeart Bell","description":"A small bronze bell with no clapper, mounted on a rusted hook near the hearth. It rings only when struck by a spoken name, and its tone causes the manor to realign in response. The sound is so intimate it feels borrowed from inside the listener's chest.","start_scene_id":"HEARTH_CHAMBER","spatial_position":"hanging from a hook above the fireplace grate"}]}

system_prompt: You are a master world-builder for a dark RPG. Your task is to generate a coherent, interconnected game world based on a provided Story Idea. The world must consist of unique scenes, connections (exits), NPCs, and interactable objects. IMPORTANT: Every NPC and Object must have a specific 'spatial_position' relative to items in the room (e.g., 'behind the bar counter', 'in the locked drawer'). Ensure the logic of the world is consistent: if a door is locked, mention why. Provide rich, atmospheric descriptions.
user_prompt:
Adventure Title: Haunted House III
Story Idea: In the shifting, non-Euclidean halls of Manor Rabenfels, a team of specialized "Extractors" is hired to recover a forbidden relic. This is not a standard ghost hunt: it is a psychological heist. The manor is a sentient predator that feeds on human memories, physically restructuring itself based on the occupants' pasts. To survive, the players must navigate distorted visions and trade their own identities for progress, eventually confronting their own mirror images. It is a race to secure the prize before the house consumes their reason for being there in the first place.

Generate at least 5 scenes with a complex network of exits and interesting entities.
response: {"scenes":[{"id":"GATECOURT","name":"Iron Gate Court","description":"A rain-slick forecourt of black cobbles and dead box hedges, enclosed by a wrought-iron gate that bends inward like ribs around a lung. Manor Rabenfels rises beyond it in too many angles at once, its windows dim as cataracts. The air tastes of old brass, wet soil, and the faint metallic sting of remembered fear. Every few heartbeats, the manor's facade seems to subtly rearrange itself, as if deciding which version of welcome is most predatory."},{"id":"ENTRY_HALL","name":"The Entry Hall of Borrowed Names","description":"A vaulted hall with a floor of cracked marble laid in a spiral that never quite reaches the center. Portraits line the walls, but the faces within them shift to resemble whoever is looking. A vast chandelier hangs overhead, lit by candles that burn with pale blue flames and drip wax that smells like medication. The hallway mutters with layered echoes, as if dozens of guests are speaking from inside the walls, each remembering the manor differently."},{"id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","name":"The Gallery of Recalled Rooms","description":"A long gallery of impossible paintings and door-shaped alcoves. Each frame shows not landscapes, but rooms the viewer once knew: a childhood kitchen, a barracks bunk, an operating theater, a lover's bedroom. The painted rooms occasionally breathe, their curtains trembling from an unseen draft. The floorboards flex softly underfoot, like the ribs of something asleep and listening."},{"id":"SERVANTS_CORRIDOR","name":"The Servants' Corridor","description":"A narrow, low-ceilinged passage behind the manor's public face. It smells of lye, dust, and old stew. Pipes run overhead like veins, and every few meters a dumbwaiter slot opens into darkness. The corridor is full of faint scratch marks, tally marks, and names carved into the plaster from people who clearly expected rescue that never came."},{"id":"OBSERVATORY","name":"The Observatory of Split Horizons","description":"A domed chamber high in the manor, open to a sky that is not always the same sky. Brass telescopes point at constellations that rearrange into faces when stared at too long. The floor is inlaid with a map of the house, though rooms on the map drift to different coordinates whenever no one is looking. Thin chains hang from the dome and clink softly, as if something invisible is pacing above the ceiling."},{"id":"CRYPT_VAULT","name":"The Subterranean Vault-Crypt","description":"A damp stone chamber beneath the manor where burial niches, filing cabinets, and reliquaries have fused into a single architecture. The walls sweat cold water that tastes faintly of ink. Rows of sealed drawers line the crypt, each labeled with names, dates, and sometimes emotions instead of contents. At the center stands a circular plinth scored by claw marks, as if the relic was once displayed here and later defended against retrieval."},{"id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","name":"The Mirror Chamber","description":"A circular room lined floor-to-ceiling with tarnished mirrors. None of them reflect the room correctly; each shows a slightly different version of the present, some with extra doors, some with missing limbs, some with the occupants replaced by older or younger selves. The center of the chamber holds a table set with silver tools and a ritual basin filled with black water that ripples whenever a lie is spoken nearby."}],"exits":[{"from_scene_id":"GATECOURT","to_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","label":"a towering front door with a lion-head knocker","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The door opens only when the visitor relinquishes a spoken memory at the threshold; the manor feeds on the offering and unlocks the latch from within."},{"from_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","to_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","label":"a paneling seam that unfolds into a velvet-draped archway","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","to_scene_id":"SERVANTS_CORRIDOR","label":"a concealed service hatch behind the grandfather clock","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","to_scene_id":"OBSERVATORY","label":"a staircase painted onto the far wall that becomes real when climbed without looking down","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","to_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","label":"a corridor of mirrors nested between the frames","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The passage only opens for someone who names a truth they have never admitted aloud; otherwise the mirrors remain flat and unyielding."},{"from_scene_id":"SERVANTS_CORRIDOR","to_scene_id":"CRYPT_VAULT","label":"a coal chute descending into a cold stone spiral","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The spiral is barred by an iron ward etched with funerary sigils, meant to keep the manor's buried names from wandering upstairs."},{"from_scene_id":"OBSERVATORY","to_scene_id":"CRYPT_VAULT","label":"a maintenance ladder sealed behind a rotating bookshelf mechanism","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"CRYPT_VAULT","to_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","label":"a subfloor arch where reflected water drips upward","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"MIRROR_CHAMBER","to_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","label":"a revolving wall that returns you to the hall when you fail to recognize your own face","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null}],"npcs":[{"id":"MARA_VOSS","type":"NPC","name":"Mara Voss, Team Lead Extractor","description":"A gaunt professional in a soot-dark coat with silver thread at the cuffs and a voice trained to sound calm even while breaking. Mara's eyes never settle for long; they keep measuring exits, distances, and lies. She carries herself like someone who has sold parts of her history before and is prepared to do it again if the payout justifies the wound.","start_scene_id":"ENTRY_HALL","spatial_position":"standing just beneath the chandelier, one hand on the banister of the spiral stair"},{"id":"DR_HELM","type":"NPC","name":"Dr. Helm, Mnemonic Specialist","description":"A thin man with ink-stained fingers and a lacquered case of surgical memory instruments. He speaks in precise, clinical phrases, yet flinches when certain names are mentioned, as if they have teeth. His spectacles are stitched with a tiny brass chain to prevent the manor from 'borrowing' them.","start_scene_id":"MEMORY_GALLERY","spatial_position":"kneeling beside the third painting from the left, peering into its frame"},{"id":"SABLE_QUIRE","type":"NPC","name":"Sable Quire, Lockbreaker and Containment Expert","description":"Broad-shouldered and restless, Sable wears a ring of old keys at her belt and a smile that appears only when something is about to fail beautifully. She keeps one gloved hand near her tools at all times. The manor seems to dislike her, which she considers encouraging.","start_scene_id":"SERVANTS_CORRIDOR","spatial_position":"crouched beside the dumbwaiter slot under the flickering gas lamp"},{"id":"ELIAS_RABEN","type":"NPC","name":"Elias Raben, Heir Apparent","description":"A young man in a mourning suit, pale as candle smoke, with an expression of polite exhaustion. He claims to be the manor's rightful heir, though he never quite says whether that is a blessing or a curse. His shadow occasionally detaches a fraction of an inch from the floor, moving with its own opinions.","start_scene_id":"OBSERVATORY","spatial_position":"standing at the brass telescope closest to the dome's north window"},{"id":"HOUSE_KEEPER","type":"NPC","name":"The House Keeper","description":"An elderly, unnervingly immaculate caretaker with a face like folded linen and hands wrapped in yellowed gloves. system_prompt: You are a master world-builder for a dark RPG. Your task is to generate a coherent, interconnected game world based on a provided Story Idea. The world must consist of unique scenes, connections (exits), NPCs, and interactable objects. IMPORTANT: Every NPC and Object must have a specific 'spatial_position' relative to items in the room (e.g., 'behind the bar counter', 'in the locked drawer'). Ensure the logic of the world is consistent: if a door is locked, mention why. Provide rich, atmospheric descriptions.
user_prompt:
Adventure Title: Haunted House V
Story Idea: In the shifting, non-Euclidean halls of Manor Rabenfels, a team of specialized "Extractors" is hired to recover a forbidden relic. This is not a standard ghost hunt: it is a psychological heist. The manor is a sentient predator that feeds on human memories, physically restructuring itself based on the occupants' pasts. To survive, the players must navigate distorted visions and trade their own identities for progress, eventually confronting their own mirror images. It is a race to secure the prize before the house consumes their reason for being there in the first place.

Generate at least 5 scenes with a complex network of exits and interesting entities.
response: {"scenes":[{"id":"MANOR_GATE","name":"Rabenfels Gatehouse","description":"A soot-black gatehouse crouches beneath iron spikes bent inward like grasping fingers. Beyond the warped gates, Manor Rabenfels rises on a hill of dead cypress, its windows shining with a weak, bruise-colored light. The air tastes of rain on old stone and something sweeter underneath, like rotting fruit left too long in a sealed room. A brass plaque on the gate is blank at first glance, but if stared at long enough, the letters seem to rearrange themselves into names the observer has forgotten."},{"id":"FOYER","name":"The Shifting Foyer","description":"The entrance hall is too large for the exterior shell, and the geometry never quite settles. A marble staircase splits and reunites midair before vanishing into a wall of portrait frames. Wallpaper blooms and withers in slow waves, patterned with the silhouettes of people standing behind their own skin. Every sound here arrives twice: once as it happens, and once as a memory of having happened somewhere else."},{"id":"LIBRARY","name":"The Memory Library","description":"Rows of towering bookcases climb into darkness, though the ceiling seems to move away whenever it is looked at directly. The books are bound in cracked leather, canvas, hair, and thin sheets of lacquered flesh. Some volumes whisper in familiar voices from the corners of your childhood, urging you to open them. A reading desk in the center holds a perpetually turning hourglass filled with black ash instead of sand."},{"id":"CONSERVATORY","name":"The Glass Conservatory","description":"A long hall of stained-glass panels and dead botanical specimens, where moonlight fractures into cold colors on the floor. Vines creep through the panes from the outside, though no garden exists on the grounds. Each plant in the room is grafted with something human: a hand-shaped lily, a rose that opens like an eye, a fern that rustles with whispered apologies. The conservatory breathes faintly, fogging the glass with each inhalation."},{"id":"CRYPT","name":"The Family Crypt","description":"Below the manor lies a chamber of damp stone and iron filings, lined with coffin niches bearing names that have been chiseled away and replaced with fresh ones. The air is stale and mineral-thick, threaded with the odor of old pennies and candle smoke. Along the far wall stands a mirror framed in funerary brass, its surface clouded except where handprints keep appearing from the other side. The crypt feels less like a burial site than a waiting room."},{"id":"HEART_CHAMBER","name":"The House Heart","description":"At the center of the manor is a room that should not exist: an organ-like chamber with walls of dark timber and pulsing mortar. Pipes descend from the ceiling like veins, carrying whispers instead of water. The floor is covered in fragments of family photographs and expedition notes, all fused together by resinous black sap. Here the manor’s intelligence becomes intimate, speaking in the remembered cadences of the intruders it has already tasted. A lacquered pedestal stands in the middle, and upon it rests the forbidden relic—or what is left of it after being dreamed through the house."},{"id":"MIRROR_GALLERY","name":"The Mirror Gallery","description":"A corridor of tall mirrors extends in impossible angles, reflecting not the room but alternate outcomes: a teammate betrayed, a doorway sealed with flesh, a child’s room where none should exist, a corpse standing up to continue the conversation. Some reflections lag behind reality by several seconds; others move before their owners do. The farther one walks, the more the mirrors begin to show the viewer as an intruder in their own life."}],"exits":[{"from_scene_id":"MANOR_GATE","to_scene_id":"FOYER","label":"the front doors beneath a cracked stone portico","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The doors are barred by a memory-lock that only opens to someone willing to surrender the name of their first home."},{"from_scene_id":"FOYER","to_scene_id":"LIBRARY","label":"a corridor of portraits whose eyes all point left","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"FOYER","to_scene_id":"CONSERVATORY","label":"a glass-paned archway overgrown with pale vines","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"FOYER","to_scene_id":"CRYPT","label":"a hidden stair behind the grand staircase's third banister","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The stair descends only after blood touches the banister, awakening the house's old burial wards."},{"from_scene_id":"LIBRARY","to_scene_id":"MIRROR_GALLERY","label":"a revolving shelf marked with a family crest","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"LIBRARY","to_scene_id":"HEART_CHAMBER","label":"a trapdoor concealed beneath the reading desk","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"The trapdoor is sealed by a cipher of stolen recollections; it opens when three memories are correctly placed into the hourglass."},{"from_scene_id":"CONSERVATORY","to_scene_id":"MIRROR_GALLERY","label":"a corridor of reflective glass panes behind the orchids","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"CRYPT","to_scene_id":"HEART_CHAMBER","label":"a service tunnel tunneled through the old foundation","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null},{"from_scene_id":"MIRROR_GALLERY","to_scene_id":"HEART_CHAMBER","label":"the central mirror that becomes a doorway when unobserved","is_locked":true,"lock_description":"It only opens if the party lets the house copy one true memory from each member and accepts the reflections in exchange."},{"from_scene_id":"HEART_CHAMBER","to_scene_id":"FOYER","label":"a collapsing passage of wet timber and plaster","is_locked":false,"lock_description":null}],"npcs":[{"id":"VARGA_EXTRACTOR","type":"NPC","name":"Captain Varga, Lead Extractor","description":"A severe woman in rain-dark leather with a surveyor's lantern hooked to her belt, her eyes sharpened by insomnia and calculation. Beneath her gloves, the fingertips are ink-stained from too many forbidden maps and too many names crossed out.","start_scene_id":"FOYER","spatial_position":"standing just inside the front door, beside the umbrella stand"},{"id":"MIREN_LOCKE","type":"NPC","name":"Miren Locke, Memory Surgeon","description":"A thin, pale specialist with a silver monacle-lens and a satchel of scalpels wrapped in velvet. Her voice is gentle enough to be mistaken for kindness until she begins asking questions that leave scars. She is fascinated by the way fear edits recall into fiction.","start_scene_id":"LIBRARY","spatial_position":"seated at the reading desk, beneath the turning ash hourglass"},{"id":"EIRA_VOSS","type":"NPC","name":"Eira Voss, Medium and Translator","description":"A febrile woman with candlewax fingers and a throat stained blue from ritual dyes, always listening as if someone just out of sight is finishing her thoughts. Rain needles down through the iron lattice of the gatecourt, bead by bead collecting on black railings, wet gravel, and the shoulders of Marek Voss’s coat. Beyond him, Manor Rabenfels looms with a patient, hungry stillness, its amber-lit windows seeming less like lamps than eyes opened in a dream. The dead yews stand in disciplined rows like mourners who have forgotten the name of the dead. Every step in the gravel is a small betrayal: the stones shift and settle behind you as if the path is already deciding what parts of your arrival to keep and what to erase.

Near the gatebell’s stone plinth, a ledger lies hidden in the base, its presence suggested more than shown, as though the manor expects its guests to notice what it has decided to reveal. Ahead, the front doors remain sealed in a way that feels ceremonial rather than secure, their raven carvings dark with old weather and older intent. To one side, the ivy-choked servants’ entrance yawns open beneath the arcade, offering a quieter way in—though in a house like this, quiet is only another form of listening. Marek stands just inside the gate, still and watchful, as if waiting to see whether the house will acknowledge you first.

response:
Zack stands before the iron gates of Rabenfels as if before the ribs of some titanic corpse, the bars black with age and slick with ivy that pulses faintly, as though the manor itself still has a heartbeat. The wind tugs at the gravel path beyond, but the path does not simply lead forward—it curves with a subtle wrongness, folding into the distance like a corridor remembered badly. Each breath brings wet stone, dust, and the faint, impossible trace of perfume long gone stale, while the gates whisper in half-formed fragments that snag at the edge of recognition, names almost—but not quite—his own.

Beyond the threshold, the manor waits in silence, its windows dim and watchful, its silhouette already beginning to feel less like a building and more like an intention. Nothing here attacks, yet the place feels predatory in the way a still pool feels deep: patient, aware, and certain of what enters.

It does not flatter; it records. If questioned honestly, it shows the most recent act of self-betrayal committed by the viewer. The frame is etched with tiny hands reaching inward.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_GALLERY","spatial_position":"at the far end of the red runner, mounted in an ornate frame"},{"id":"MEMORY_BOOK","type":"OBJECT","name":"Blank Memory Codex","description":"A large book of ash-gray pages that remain blank until a personal recollection is spoken near it. Once written into, the text becomes difficult to remember accurately, as if the book is feeding on the event it records. Several pages have teeth marks along the edges.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_LIBRARY","spatial_position":"wedged halfway out from the third shelf on the west wall"},{"id":"BUTCHER_KEY","type":"OBJECT","name":"Butcher's Key","description":"A heavy iron key with a handle shaped like a cleaver's spine. It smells of salt, marrow, and old vinegar. The key was made to open things that should not remain closed, and it leaves a greasy stain on the hand that carries it.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_GALLEY","spatial_position":"hanging from a hook beside the cold hearth"},{"id":"REDBIND_LADDER","type":"OBJECT","name":"The Red-Bound Service Ladder","description":"A narrow folding ladder wrapped in red cord, used by servants to reach vents, ducts, and upper cabinets. Its rungs are worn smooth by hurried climbs. When unfolded, it points insistently toward the places the house is trying to hide.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_GALLEY","spatial_position":"leaning against the pantry hatch"},{"id":"DESCENT_LEVER","type":"OBJECT","name":"Hidden Descent Lever","description":"A brass lever set into the back of the gallery wall, disguised as a decorative flourish until touched. It is warm from use, and its mechanism hums with reluctant motion. Pulling it feels like convincing the house to swallow a secret.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_GALLERY","spatial_position":"behind the last portrait on the right, concealed in the molding"},{"id":"MIRROR_SHARD","type":"OBJECT","name":"Shard of Self","description":"A jagged fragment of mirror glass that reflects a different expression each time it is turned. It is sharp enough to cut skin, memory, or certainty. The shard vibrates softly near the relic, like a compass for identity.","start_scene_id":"RABENFELS_VAULT","spatial_position":"embedded in a crack in the wall near the plinth"}]}

system_prompt:
You are the Gamemaster (GM) of an AI Text Adventure RPG. You dynamically generate world narratives, resolve choices, and act as NPCs. The world context/setting is:
In the shifting, non-Euclidean halls of Manor Rabenfels, a team of specialized "Extractors" is hired to recover a forbidden relic. This is not a standard ghost hunt: it is a psychological heist. The manor is a sentient predator that feeds on human memories, physically restructuring itself based on the occupants' pasts. To survive, the players must navigate distorted visions and trade their own identities for progress, eventually confronting their own mirror images. It is a race to secure the prize before the house consumes their reason for being there in the first place.

CURRENT GAME TIME: Day 1, 08:05

--- CURRENT LOCATION ---
NAME: The Gatecourt of Manor Rabenfels (ID: RABENFELS_GATECOURT)
DESCRIPTION: A rain-slick forecourt of black flagstones and wrought iron, enclosed by walls that seem taller when unobserved. The manor rises beyond them like a patient animal crouched on bone-white foundations. Every window is dark except for occasional, impossible flickers of life behind the glass, as though the house is remembering someone who never lived here. The air tastes of old brass, wet wool, and a faint copper tang that suggests the building has been feeding recently.
PRESENT NPCs: Mara Voss, Team Lead Extractor (Position: standing just inside the iron gate, with one hand on the latch)
AVAILABLE EXITS:
- the main oak doors, swollen as if breathed on from inside to RABENFELS_ENTRY_HALL [OPEN]

Below is the REAL-TIME character sheet of the player. You MUST consider these stats, equipment, and HP in all your narratives:

}

Respond organically. Use the pre-generated world description as the static reality. If an exit is LOCKED, the player cannot pass unless they find a way to unlock it.
TIME ADVANCEMENT: Some complex actions take extra time. You can specify `extra_time_minutes` in your response.

response:
Rain needles down across the gatecourt, hissing when it strikes the black flagstones. Manor Rabenfels looms ahead with the awful stillness of something pretending to sleep, its windows dark until they aren’t—brief, wrong flickers of motion skating behind the glass like remembered faces trying to find their way back in. The iron gate stands half-open in Mara Voss’s grip, and she doesn’t look at you so much as measure you, her posture fixed and ready as if the house itself might lunge the moment her hand slips. The air tastes of wet wool, old brass, and a coppery note that clings to the back of the throat.

Beyond the gate, the main doors wait under the manor’s overhang, swollen and inward-breathing, as though something on the other side has been pressing politely against the wood. Around you, the walls seem to lengthen whenever you try not to stare at them, and the forecourt feels less like a place than a held breath. Mara finally shifts her weight, still keeping the latch, and says quietly, “Eyes open. Don’t trust the first version of anything in here.”
